title: Security Tools
description: JSON Schema definitions for security tools available to the ideas-brillantes assistant. Provides malware scanning for files and URLs, process inspection, file quarantine, security report generation, and network traffic monitoring.

tools.name: scan_file
tools.description: Scan a file or directory for malware, suspicious signatures, or known threats using the configured antivirus engine.

path.description: Absolute path to the file or directory to scan.

deep.description: If true, performs a deep/heuristic scan (slower but more thorough). Defaults to a fast signature scan.

tools.name: scan_url
tools.description: Check a URL against threat intelligence databases and sandbox analysis to detect malware, phishing, or other threats.

tools.name: check_process
tools.description: Inspect one or more running processes for suspicious behaviour, unusual network connections, or known malware indicators.

pid.description: Optional PID of a specific process to inspect. If omitted and name is also omitted, all running processes are checked.

name.description: Optional process name substring to match (e.g. 'chrome', 'python'). Can be combined with pid.

tools.name: quarantine_file
tools.description: Move a suspicious file to the quarantine vault, making it inaccessible while preserving it for later analysis or recovery.

path.description: Absolute path to the file to quarantine.

tools.name: get_security_report
tools.description: Generate a summary security report covering scan results, quarantined items, and flagged events since a given time.

since.description: Optional ISO 8601 timestamp. Only events occurring after this time are included. If omitted, reports on all recorded events.

tools.name: monitor_network
tools.description: Start capturing and analysing network traffic on a given interface for a short duration, returning a summary of suspicious or notable connections.

interface.description: Network interface name to monitor (e.g. 'eth0', 'wlan0'). Defaults to the primary active interface.

filter.description: Optional BPF (Berkeley Packet Filter) expression to narrow captured traffic (e.g. 'port 443', 'host 192.168.1.1').
